content.full_text:
A-82-0110

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 307 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1160 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5665 322-5660 322-5991 322-6441 322-6444

February 8, 1982


Nancy Miller, Esq.
Law Offices of Richard Hyde
111 Capitol Mall Bldg.; Suite 225
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Paratransit, Inc.

Dear Nancy:

Thank you for sending me the Agreement between the City
of Sacramento, County of Sacramento, Sacramento Regional
Transit District, and Sacramento Area Council of Governments
regarding Paratransit, Inc.

We have reviewed our prior determination that Paratransit,
Inc., was not a local governmental agency within the meaning
of the Political Reform Act. In light of the recent changes
to Paratransit's governing structure, we have now concluded
that Paratransit, Inc., is a local governmental agency and
is therefore required to adopt a conflict of interest code.

As a result of the above-mentioned Agreement, our under-
standing is that two basic things happened. First, Paratransit
was given additional responsibilities through the expansion
of its services to a larger population. As part of this,
Paratransit has taken over the operation of Regional Transit's
Careful Coach. Second, Paratransit's Board of Directors
must now be composed of persons appointed by the governmental
entities who were parties to the Agreement.

Since these changes to Paratransit represent initiative
by governmental entities to provide for and direct the
basic governmental service of transportation to members of
the public, the criteria suggested in the Siegel opinion1/
for the identification of a local governmental agency are
satisfied. These criteria include:

___________________________
1/Opinion requested by Samuel Siegel, 3 FPPC Opinions
62 (No. 76-054, July 6, 1977).

Nancy Miller, Esq.
February 8, 1982
Page Two


     (1)  Whether the impetus for formation of the corporation
originated with a government agency;

     (2)  Whether it is substantially funded by, or its primary
source of funds is, a government agency;

     (3)  Whether one of the principal purposes for which it
is formed is to provide services or undertake obligations
which public agencies are legally authorized to perform
and which, in fact, they traditionally have performed; and

     (4)  Whether the corporation is treated as a public
entity by other statutory provisions.

     In effect, the changes to Paratransit's structure and
functions caused the creation of a new entity at the impetus
of government agencies.  Also Paratransit is substantially
funded by a government agency, i.e., SACOG.  Its principal
purpose is to provide transportation services which public
agencies are legally authorized to perform and traditionally
have performed.  Based on all of these factors, we think that
Paratransit is intrinsically "public" in character.  Its
members are "public officials" within the meaning of Government
Code Sections 82048 and 87100, and Paratransit is a government
agency within the meaning of Government Code Section 82041.

     Please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5501 if you
would like to discuss this letter or if you have further
questions.

     The deadline for the submission of a conflict of interest
code for Paratransit is August 1, 1982.

                                    Very truly yours,

                                    [signature]

                                    Diane Maura Fishburn
                                    Counsel
                                    Legal Division

DMF:plh

LAW OFFICES
OF
RICHARD H. HYDE INC.
1111 Capitol Mall Building Suite 225
Sacramento, California 95814
Telephone: (916) 447-7933

MEMORANDUM


TO: Diane Maura Fishburn                    Dated: 1/28/82

RE: Paratransit, Inc.
    Designation as a Public or Private agency for FPPC Purposes


     As per your request, enclosed please find a copy of the
contract designating new board selection criteria for Paratransit,
Inc.

                                        Nancy C. Miller

Third Draft                                                June 26, 1981


FOUR PARTY AGREEMENT RELATING TO THE PROVISION OF
TRANSPORTATION SERVICES TO THE ELDERLY AND HANDICAPPED


THIS AGREEMENT between CITY OF SACRAMENTO, a charter city

(CITY); the COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO, a charter county (COUNTY);

SACRAMENTO REGIONAL TRANSIT DISTRICT, a public corporation (RT);

and SACRAMENTO AREA COUNCIL OF GOVERNMENTS, a joint powers

agency (SACOG), is entered into on this _____ day of June, 1991.


R E C I T A L S


WHEREAS, California Government Code Section 15975 requires

SACOG to adopt an action plan relating to transportation services

provided to social service recipients, which plan must include

but not be limited to, the designation of a Consolidated Trans-

portation Service Agency (CTSA) within the geographic area of

the jurisdiction of the transportation planning agency; and


WHEREAS, the action plan may designate more than a single

agency or multiple agencies as Consolidated Transportation

Service Agencies and must specify an orderly strategy and

schedule detailing the steps required to develop the financial

program and management structure necessary to implement consoli-

dated or coordinated services; and


                                                                    1

WHEREAS, SACOG has prepared a Draft AB120 Action Plan for

Consolidation of Social Service Transportation dated June, 1981

which recognizes Paratransit Inc. (PTI) as an eligible Consoli-

dated Transportation Service Agency [AB 120 refers to Social Service

Transportation Improvement Act (Chapter 1120 , Statutes of 1979)] ; and


WHEREAS, presently transportation services to the elderly

and handicapped are provided both by RT and Paratransit, Inc., a

non-profit corporation; and


WHEREAS, it is the intent of the parties to balance the

following objectives in providing transportation services to

the elderly and handicapped:

     1.   To centralize transportation services to the elderly

and handicapped with a single agency; and

     2.   To assure that such service is monitored by a responsible

public entity; and


WHEREAS, PTI is an eligible designee as a Consolidated

Transportation Service Agency (CTSA); and


WHEREAS, in the event of a designation of PTI as a CTSA,

it will be necessary for each of the parties to this agreement

to contribute in some manner in order to accomplish consolidation of all

transportation services to the elderly and handicapped with

PTI; and


2

WHEREAS, it is the purpose of this agreement to set forth

the terms and conditions under which their contribution will be

made.

WITNESSETH:

I.    TERM

     This agreement shall be for a one-year term and shall

expire on June 30, 1982.  This agreement shall be null and void

in the event SACOG does not designate PTI as a CTSA on or before

July 31, 1981.

II.  DESIGNATION:  PRECONDITIONS:

     As a precondition to SACOG's designation of PTI as a CTSA,

PTI or any other non-profit agency applying for status as a

CTSA shall do the following:

     A.  Agree in writing not to be an applicant for funds made

          available by Article 4 of the Transportation Development

          Act nor for funds made available by the State Transit

          Assistance Program established by Chapter 161 of the

          Statutes of 1979 (SB 620) except for grants for specific

          projects upon approval of RT or SACOG; and

     B.  Establish a board of directors which shall serve at the

          pleasure of the appointing authority mentioned for each

          appointment below and shall serve staggered terms, but

          in no event shall any single term exceed four years.

3

Board Qualifications

1. Be made up of nine members.

2. Two members, appointed by the City Council, who shall
be representative of the general public (non users of
the service).

3. Two members, appointed by the County Board of Supervisors,
who shall be representatives of the general public
(non users of the service).

4. Two members, appointed by the Board of Directors of RT.

5. One member, appointed by SACOG, representing any city
or county with whom PTI contracts for service.

6. Two members, one appointed by the Sacramento City
Council and one by the Sacramento Board of Supervisors,
representing the user community.


III. CITY OF SACRAMENTO

CITY will pay funds in the amount of $650,000.00 to SACOG
to pass through to the appropriate agencies only upon the
conditions set forth in this agreement to which all parties
to this agreement agree to be mutually bound.

It is mutually understood by all parties that the CITY
agreement to contribute funds relates to a period of one year
only. Any contribution beyond June 30, 1982, by CITY will
require affirmative action of the Sacramento City Council.

4

LAW OFFICES
OF
RICHARD H. HYDE, INC.
111 Capitol Mall Building, Suite 225
Sacramento, California 95814
Telephone: (916) 447-7933

DEC 21 11 34 AM 01

December 16, 1981

Diane Fishburn
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Paratransit, Inc.

Dear Ms. Fishburn:

Your letter of November 24, 1981, (enclosed) determined
that Paratransit, Inc. is a private Agency, and therefore is
not subject to FPPC Regulations regarding the preparation of
a conflict of interest code.

The Bylaws of Paratransit Inc. were amended pursuant to
an Agreement between Paratransit, Inc., Regional Transit, the
City of Sacramento and SACOG (Sacramento Area Council of
Governments). Under the current bylaws, all board members
are appointed by the governing bodies of local government
entities (the City and County of Sacramento, Regional Transit
and SACOG). In addition, two members of the nine member
Paratransit Board of Directors are concurrently members of
the Regional Transit Board of Directors.

Do either of these facts alter your initial determination
that Paratransit, Inc. is a private agency?

Please contact me if you have any questions regarding
this matter.

Very truly yours,

RICHARD H. HYDE, INC.

BY [signature]
NANCY C. MILLER

NCM:cr

Enclosure

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance          Administration          Executive/Legal          Enforcement          Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662              322-5660              322-5901              272-0441              322-0444

November 24, 1981


Nancy Miller, Esquire
Law Offices of Richard A. Hyde, Inc.
111 Capitol Mall Bldg., Suite 225
Sacramento, CA 95814

                                                    Re: Paratransit, Inc.

Dear Nancy:

     Thank you for your recent request for advice as to whether
Paratransit, Inc., is a local government agency and as such,
subject to the provisions of the Political Reform Act.

     We agree with your conclusion that Paratransit, Inc., is
not a local government agency and that its board members are
not public officials.

     This conclusion is based on an application of the Siegel
criteria,1/ and our understanding of the following facts as
stated in your letter. Paratransit, Inc., is a private nonprofit
corporation formed and directed by private persons. Paratransit's
purpose is to provide transit services to persons with mobility
handicaps. Funding for Paratransit comes primarily from contracts
with local, state and federal entities and partially from the
collection of fares. Paratransit employees are not public
employees nor does the Paratransit Board have any advisory role
with regard to the expenditure of public funds.

     If I can be of further assistance, please feel free to
contact me or Susan Harrigan of our office at (916) 322-5901.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                [signature]
                                                Diane Maura Fishburn
                                                Counsel
                                                Legal Division
DMF:plh

     1/ Opinion requested by Samuel Siegel, 3 FPPC Opinions 62
(No. 76-054, July 6, 1977).

LAW OFFICES
OF
RICHARD H. HYDE, INC.
111 Capitol Mall Building, Suite 225
Sacramento, California 95814
Telephone: (916) 447-7933

October 23, 1981


Diane Fishburn, Esquire
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Paratransit, Inc.

Dear Diane:

     Although you are currently on vacation, I am sending
you this letter regarding a client of our firm, Paratransit,
Inc., who provides bus transportation services to handicapped
and elderly residents.  I am unsure as to whether the board
is subject to the Fair Political Practices Act and would
like to discuss it with you.

     Paratransit, Inc. is a private non-profit corporation
which was organized and is directed by private persons (as
opposed to public employees).  The purpose of the corporation
is to provide transit services to persons with mobility
handicaps.  Paratransit not only has its own fleet of vans
but it also maintains a repair shop for the repair of other
agencies' vehicles (i.e. United Cerebral Palsy, Sacramento
Association for the Retarded).

     Paratransit, Inc. is funded primarily by public money
and partially through fares it collects from riders.  The
public money is obtained by contract with local, state or
federal entities.  Paratransit employees are not public
employees nor does the Paratransit Board have any advisory
role with regards to the expenditure of public funds.  Para-
transit, Inc. prepares grant proposals which it submits to
a public agency or entity and competes for grants with other
non-profit groups.

Page Two
Fishburn
10/23/81


     In my review of FPPC opinions, it is my conclusion
that Paratransit, Inc. is neither a "local public agency"
nor are its board members "public officials" within the
meaning of the Fair Political Practices Act.  I would like
to discuss this with you, and if necessary request a formal
opinion.

     I will not be in the office for the next few weeks,
but Dick Hyde will contact you regarding your thoughts on
this matter.  In the meantime, if you have any questions,
please do not hesitate to phone Dick.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        NANCY C. MILLER

NCM:cr

sections.question: Do either of these facts alter your initial determination that Paratransit, Inc. is a private agency? [Facts: (1) All board members are appointed by governing bodies of local government entities; (2) Two members of the nine member Paratransit Board are concurrently members of the Regional Transit Board of Directors]
sections.conclusion: We have reviewed our prior determination that Paratransit, Inc., was not a local governmental agency within the meaning of the Political Reform Act. In light of the recent changes to Paratransit's governing structure, we have now concluded that Paratransit, Inc., is a local governmental agency and is therefore required to adopt a conflict of interest code. Since these changes to Paratransit represent initiative by governmental entities to provide for and direct the basic governmental service of transportation to members of the public, the criteria suggested in the Siegel opinion for the identification of a local governmental agency are satisfied. Based on all of these factors, we think that Paratransit is intrinsically 'public' in character. Its members are 'public officials' within the meaning of Government Code Sections 82048 and 87100, and Paratransit is a government agency within the meaning of Government Code Section 82041.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether changes to Paratransit, Inc.'s board composition—including appointment of all directors by local government entities and overlap with Regional Transit board membership—alter the FPPC's prior determination that Paratransit is a private agency not subject to the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC reversed its prior determination and concluded that Paratransit, Inc. is now a local governmental agency subject to the Political Reform Act, based on the structural changes giving governmental entities control over board appointments and directing public transportation services. Paratransit board members are therefore public officials required to adopt a conflict of interest code by August 1, 1982.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This document is a compilation of multiple letters showing the progression of the inquiry: initial request (10/23/81), FPPC initial determination (11/24/81 - finding private agency), follow-up question with new facts (12/16/81), and FPPC reversal (2/8/82 - finding public agency). The main FPPC response is the 2/8/82 letter reversing prior advice. Some supporting documents included (four-party agreement, memoranda) provide context for the changed circumstances.

[Facts: (1) All board members are appointed by governing bodies of local government entities; (2) Two members of the nine member Paratransit Board are concurrently members of the Regional Transit Board of Directors]

CONCLUSION: We have reviewed our prior determination that Paratransit, Inc., was not a local governmental agency within the meaning of the Political Reform Act. In light of the recent changes to Paratransit's governing structure, we have now concluded that Paratransit, Inc., is a local governmental agency and is therefore required to adopt a conflict of interest code. Since these changes to Paratransit represent initiative by governmental entities to provide for and direct the basic governmental service of transportation to members of the public, the criteria suggested in the Siegel opinion for the identification of a local governmental agency are satisfied. Based on all of these factors, we think that Paratransit is intrinsically 'public' in character. Its members are 'public officials' within the meaning of Government Code Sections 82048 and 87100, and Paratransit is a government agency within the meaning of Government Code Section 82041.

embedding.summary: The FPPC initially determined Paratransit, Inc. was a private agency not subject to the Political Reform Act, but reversed this determination upon learning that a new agreement made all board members appointed by local government entities. The FPPC concluded Paratransit is now a local governmental agency whose members are public officials, applying the Siegel criteria for governmental agency identification.
